verdicts.reason: The cited call site exists verbatim at appclient.go:240 inside the http.StatusUnauthorized branch of providerError, which takes no context.Context parameter; all seven callers of providerError (DeleteWebhook, GetAccount, GetPaymentMethod, GetInvoice, CreateInvoice, UpdateInvoice, DeleteInvoice, etc.) receive ctx as a parameter but cannot thread it through, forcing UpdateAppStatus to execute outside the request context with context.Background() and severing OTel spans and transaction semantics, violating the documented pf-008 and ctx-propagation rules.

Code at event_query.go:25 confirms sb.IfNotExists() pattern; the three hand-maintained column lists (RawEvent struct ch:-tags, createEventsTable.toSQL() Define() calls lines 26-36, InsertEventsQuery.ToSQL() Cols() line 207) are verified in the provided code; CLAUDE.md data-rawevent-clickhouse-column-sync rule and pf-010 pitfall explicitly document this exact problem, confirming it is a known architectural risk in the codebase.

The quoted call site exists at customer.go:59-61 applying filter.ApplyToQuery to name/primary_email/key fields; these filters generate leading-wildcard ILIKE patterns (per filter.go evidence and enforcement rule pf-009), the schema only declares btree indexes on these columns, and the v3 endpoint exposes the filters without a pg_trgm GIN migration prerequisite, causing sequential scans on every filtered list call.

The quoted code at cmd/billing-worker/main.go:86 calls app.NamespaceManager.GetDefaultNamespace() and passes the result to EnsureBusinessAccounts without verifying the namespace was actually provisioned by namespace.Manager handlers (which are registered only in cmd/server and called before initNamespace); if billing-worker starts before server, the namespace string exists but backend resources (ClickHouse table, Kafka topics, ledger accounts) may not, causing runtime failures with no compile-time guard.

The cited default case exists at lines 141-142 in eventbus.go and returns SystemEventsTopic unconditionally without validating that EventName matches a registered subsystem prefix. While the pattern is documented in pf_0007 and enforcement rules warn against it, the code itself has no validation to prevent misnamed event families from silently routing to the wrong topic, making this a real structural vulnerability.

The quoted call site exists at cmd/billing-worker/main.go:86. The failure mode is real: cmd/billing-worker calls EnsureBusinessAccounts without first registering namespace handlers or asserting the default namespace exists, violating the documented pattern (pf-006a) that worker binaries must fail-fast verify default namespace provisioning before namespace-scoped operations. If billing-worker starts before cmd/server, the ledger and Kafka ingest handlers never register, leaving the default namespace incompletely provisioned when EnsureBusinessAccounts attempts to write business accounts.

The quoted code exists at openmeter/server/server.go:214 (off by one line from the citation); it does call context.Background() inside the kin-openapi ErrorHandler callback which lacks access to the request context due to library API constraints, severing request-scoped context from validation error responses and violating the project's ctx-003 context propagation rule. The violation is real despite the engineer's acknowledged comment about library limitations.

The triggering call site exists and is confirmed to be part of the schema_level dual-write mechanism; GetInvoiceDefaultSchemaLevel → SetSchemaLevel creates coupled cleanup debt across the BillingInvoice schema and adapter that must be removed in lockstep, mirroring the documented pitfall pf-011.

The cited call site `customerService.RegisterHooks(h)` at app/common/customer.go:73 exists within NewCustomerLedgerServiceHook. The three hook providers (NewCustomerLedgerServiceHook, NewCustomerSubjectServiceHook, NewCustomerEntitlementValidatorServiceHook) are defined in customer.go but are not included in any wire.NewSet—the Customer set contains only NewCustomerService, and Wire has no type dependency on these hook providers' return types, so Wire will not call them and their side-effect registrations will silently not run, matching the pf_0006 failure mode exactly.

The quoted code at grouphandler.go:48-54 definitely exists and silently returns nil when a ce_type is not found in typeHandlerMap. The enforcement rule notification-001 confirms payload versioning is a design concern and requires migration paths, which implies version constants exist alongside event definitions. If notification event ce_types encode payload versions, the silent drop behavior here would silently lose events during producer/consumer version mismatches, violating visibility requirements—a valid architectural risk even if I cannot confirm ce_types actually encode versions from the provided code excerpt.
